---
title: "Predictive Modeling"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    html-math-method: katex
    code-overflow: wrap
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: false
---

Using machine learning methods to predict job growth trends.
```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession

# Start a Spark session
spark = SparkSession.builder.config("spark.driver.host", "localhost").appName("JobPostingsAnalysis").getOrCreate()
spark.catalog.clearCache()

# Load the CSV file into a Spark DataFrame
df = spark.read.option("header", "true").option("inferSchema", "true").option(
    "multiLine", "true").option("escape", "\"").csv("./data/clean_job_postings.csv")

# Register the DataFrame as a temporary SQL view
df.createOrReplaceTempView("clean_job_postings")

# Show Schema and Sample Data
#print("---This is Diagnostic check, No need to print it in the final doc---")

# comment the lines below when rendering the submission
#df.printSchema()
#df.show(5)
```
# Filtering and Feature Selection
```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
from pyspark.sql import functions as F

topic_keywords = [
    "data scientist",
    "data analyst",
    "business analyst",
    "business intelligence",
    "machine learning",
    "analytics"
]

condition = None
for kw in topic_keywords:
    c = F.lower(F.col("SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION")).contains(kw)
    condition = c if condition is None else (condition | c)

df_reg = (
    df
    .filter(F.col("SALARY").isNotNull())
    .filter(F.col("SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION").isNotNull())
    .filter(condition)
)

print("Filtered rows:", df_reg.count())
df_reg.select("SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION", "SALARY").show(20, truncate=False)
```
```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
skill_features = [
    "Python (Programming Language)",
    "SQL (Programming Language)",
    "Microsoft Excel",
    "Data Analysis",
    "Machine Learning",
    "Power BI",
    "Tableau (Business Intelligence Software)"
]

df_reg = df_reg.withColumn(
    "ALL_SKILLS_TEXT",
    F.concat_ws(
        " ",
        F.coalesce(F.col("SOFTWARE_SKILLS_NAME"), F.lit("")),
        F.coalesce(F.col("SPECIALIZED_SKILLS_NAME"), F.lit("")),
        F.coalesce(F.col("COMMON_SKILLS_NAME"), F.lit(""))
    )
)
for skill in skill_features:
    safe_name = (
        skill.replace(" ", "_")
        .replace("(", "")
        .replace(")", "")
        .replace("/", "_")
        .replace("-", "_")
    )
    
    df_reg = df_reg.withColumn(
        safe_name,
        F.when(F.col("ALL_SKILLS_TEXT").contains(skill), 1).otherwise(0)
    )

df_reg.select(
    "SALARY",
    "Python_Programming_Language",
    "SQL_Programming_Language",
    "Microsoft_Excel",
    "Data_Analysis",
    "Machine_Learning",
    "Power_BI",
    "Tableau_Business_Intelligence_Software"
).show(5)
```
```{python}
# | echo: true
# | eval: false

df_reg = df_reg.withColumn(
    "MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE_NUM",
    F.when(
        F.col("MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE").rlike("^[0-9]+(\\.[0-9]+)?$"),
        F.col("MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE").cast("double")
    ).otherwise(0.0)
)

df_reg = df_reg.fillna({
    "REMOTE_TYPE_NAME": "Not Listed",
    "MIN_EDULEVELS_NAME": "Not Listed"
})

df_reg.select(
    "MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE",
    "MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE_NUM",
    "REMOTE_TYPE_NAME",
    "MIN_EDULEVELS_NAME"
).show(10, truncate=False)
```
# Pipeline for ML
```{python}
# | echo: true
# | eval: false


from pyspark.ml.feature import StringIndexer, OneHotEncoder, VectorAssembler
from pyspark.ml import Pipeline

skill_cols_clean = [
    "Python_Programming_Language",
    "SQL_Programming_Language",
    "Microsoft_Excel",
    "Data_Analysis",
    "Machine_Learning",
    "Power_BI",
    "Tableau_Business_Intelligence_Software"
]

categorical_cols = ["REMOTE_TYPE_NAME", "MIN_EDULEVELS_NAME"]
numeric_cols = ["MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE_NUM"] + skill_cols_clean

indexers = [
    StringIndexer(inputCol=col, outputCol=f"{col}_idx", handleInvalid="keep")
    for col in categorical_cols
]

encoders = [
    OneHotEncoder(inputCol=f"{col}_idx", outputCol=f"{col}_vec")
    for col in categorical_cols
]

assembler = VectorAssembler(
    inputCols=numeric_cols + [f"{col}_vec" for col in categorical_cols],
    outputCol="features"
)
```
# Train-Test Split
```{python}
# | echo: true
# | eval: false

from pyspark.ml.regression import LinearRegression

train_df, test_df = df_reg.randomSplit([0.8, 0.2], seed=42)

lr = LinearRegression(
    featuresCol="features",
    labelCol="SALARY",
    predictionCol="prediction"
)

pipeline = Pipeline(stages=indexers + encoders + [assembler, lr])

lr_model = pipeline.fit(train_df)
predictions = lr_model.transform(test_df)

predictions.select("SALARY", "prediction").show(10, truncate=False)
```
```{python}
# | echo: true
# | eval: false
from pyspark.ml.evaluation import RegressionEvaluator

rmse_evaluator = RegressionEvaluator(
    labelCol="SALARY",
    predictionCol="prediction",
    metricName="rmse"
)

r2_evaluator = RegressionEvaluator(
    labelCol="SALARY",
    predictionCol="prediction",
    metricName="r2"
)

mae_evaluator = RegressionEvaluator(
    labelCol="SALARY",
    predictionCol="prediction",
    metricName="mae"
)

rmse = rmse_evaluator.evaluate(predictions)
r2 = r2_evaluator.evaluate(predictions)
mae = mae_evaluator.evaluate(predictions)

print("RMSE:", round(rmse, 2))
print("MAE:", round(mae, 2))
print("R-squared:", round(r2, 4))
```
```{python}
# | echo: true
# | eval: false

```
